data.title: Williams CEO pledges 'very large lawsuit' against Energy Transfer in pipeline fight
data.description:
Williams (NYSE:WMB) plans to bring a "very large lawsuit" against Energy Transfer (ET) after a legal dispute between the companies delayed construction of the Louisiana Energy Gateway project, CEO Alan Armstrong told Argus in an interview on Tuesday.

Energy Transfer (ET) is the only company in "pipeline history" to have defied industry norms over pipeline crossings in a bid to block competitors' projects, Armstrong told Argus, and he hopes the lawsuit would "stop the industry from spiraling into that kind of behavior."

Energy Transfer (ET) tried to block Williams (WMB) and other pipeline companies from building new gas pipelines across its own Tiger pipeline in Louisiana's Haynesville shale, arguing that projects by Williams and others proposed an excessive number of crossings under and over its own pipelines; opponents argued Energy Transfer was just attempting to control market share.

Beyond trying to block Williams (WMB) from crossing the Tiger pipeline, Energy Transfer (ET) also sought a federal review of Williams' proposed 1.8B cf/day Louisiana Energy Gateway pipeline as an interstate transmission line, rather than a gathering line.

Energy Transfer (ET) lost cases to Williams (WMB) in lawsuits spanning parishes across Louisiana, but the litigation pushed back Louisiana Energy Gateway's in-service date from late 2024 to H2 2025.

data.title: IGM Financial reports a 16.4% Y/Y increase in November AUM and advisement
data.description:
• Canada-based asset manager IGM Financial (TSX:IGM:CA) reported assets under management and advisement of $273.4B at November 2024-end, compared to $234.9B at the end of November 30, 2023, and $264.8B in the prior month-end.
  • The company reported net inflows of $50M during November 2024, compared to outflows of $396M in November 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thunderbird Entertainment Group announces commencement of normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Thunderbird Entertainment Group (OTCQX:THBRF) has received approval from the TSX Venture Exchange of its notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid.
  • The company may purchase for cancellation up to 3.19M common shares representing ~10% of its public float over a twelve-month period commencing on December 9, 2024.
  • The normal course issuer bid will expire no later than December 8, 2025.
  • The company has an existing normal course issuer bid in effect, which began on December 7, 2023 and is set to expire on December 6, 2024. THBRF has repurchased for cancellation 591,400 common shares for total consideration of $1.23M, representing an average price of $2.08 per common share.
  • The company intends to fund the purchases out of available cash.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Absci a new buy at Jones on AI platform to design antibodies
data.description:
• Jones has initiated Absci (NASDAQ:ABSI) at buy citing the drug creation company's AI platform that is used to create biologic drugs.
  • The firm has a $9 price target (~201% upside based on Dec. 3 close).
  • Analyst Debanjana Chatterjee said that Absci differentiates itself from rivals "with its large scale proprietary training data, zero-shot generative AI model for de novo antibody design, and integrated high throughput in house wet lab screening and validation capacity."
  • Adding to her bullishness is that the company Absci has already signed several deals with other drug companies, and has demonstrated design milestones, an achievement that has validated its technology. "Advancement of partnered products will not only generate meaningful cash flows through milestone payments and royalties, but also likely attract further partnerships."
  • Chatterjee highlighted Absci's lead asset, ABS-101 for inflammatory bowel disease, as the best-in-class anti-TL1A. She added that TL1A is a clinically validated target and ABS-101 could potentially bring in $4B in peak revenue. First-in-human data is expected in H2 2025.

data.title: NLS Pharmaceutics announces pricing of private placement of up to $1M
data.description:
• NLS Pharmaceutics (NASDAQ:NLSP) announced the pricing of a private placement offering consisting of the issuance and sale of up to 322,580 common shares at a purchase price of $3.10 per common share, for gross proceeds of up to $1M.
  • The initial closing of the offering in the amount of $500,000 is expected to occur on or before January 10, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes.
  • NLSP shares were down 3.2% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CHARBONE Hydrogen secures initial tranche of US$6M in convertible notes
data.description:
• CHARBONE Hydrogen (TSXV:CH:CA) announced on Wednesday that it has closed a $2.1M CAD unsecured convertible debt tranche to accelerate the funding of the construction of its first two green hydrogen production facilities.
  • The Sorel-Tracy facility will serve as CHARBONE's flagship green hydrogen production site, with operations scheduled to begin in the coming weeks, following delivery of a pre-ordered electrolyzer in Q1 2025.
  • The first tranche of the financing includes US$1.5 million (CA$2.1M) in gross proceeds through unsecured convertible notes with a 36-month term at a 12% annual interest rate, led by its US banker, maturing in December 2027.
  • The note offering closing of US$1.5 million(CA$2.1M)is part of a private placement led by its US banker of an aggregate principal amount up to US$6 million unsecured convertible notes for 36 months which shall bear interest at 12% per annum.

data.title: Zeta Global shares jump on huge boost in marketing platform engagement
data.description:
• Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) shares rose more than 9% on Wednesday after the marketing software company said it saw "record-setting engagement" to kick off the holiday shopping period.
  • From Thanksgiving to Cyber Monday, Zeta said its marketing platform saw a 108% year-over-year rise in usage, as companies turned to Zeta to aid in their marketing across various channels.
  • “With the holiday season being five days shorter, marketers are under increasing pressure to execute strategies that drive both value and high-impact results,” said Zeta Global Chief Executive and co-founder David Steinberg in a statement. “The Zeta Marketing Platform continues to empower brands to engage high-intent shoppers where they want to be reached, delivering exceptional efficiency and more effective outcomes during the most critical shopping days of the year.”

data.title: U.S. economic activity rises slightly since late October: Fed's Beige Book
data.description:
• Economic activity in the U.S. rose slightly across most of the Federal Reserve's 12 districts since late October, the central bank said in its latest Beige Book report.
  • That compared with the little changed economic activity across all districts in the previous Beige Book.
  • Three regions exhibited modest or moderate growth that offset flat or slightly declining activity in two others, the report noted.

data.title: Robinhood CEO Tenev 'keenly looking' into sports betting
data.description:
• Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev on Wednesday said his company is "keenly looking" into the sports betting space.
  • At the company's investor day event, Tenev said customers are interested in sports event contracts betting.
  • Sports betting in the U.S. generated revenue north of $11B in 2023. Up until 2018, Nevada was the only state providing the service, but now it is legal in 38 states and the District of Columbia, with active legislation surrounding sports betting in six more states.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell confident on having a good relationship with new Treasury secretary
data.description:
"I am confident that I'll have the same kind of relationship with him (Scott Bessent), once he's confirmed, that I've had with other Treasury secretaries," Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said Wednesday during a New York Times event.

The Fed Reserve Chair and Treasury secretary traditionally have lunch periodically. A good relationship is important because Fed officials and elected officials need to work together when dealing with crises, he said.

He declined to comment on President-elect Trump's previous comments about the Federal Reserve.

Updated at 2:00 PM ET: Powell emphasized that the central bank needs to be open in explaining officials' thinking on policy.

"We should be telling people what we're doing and why," he said.

Breaking news... check back for updates.
